text:
GROUP K WRAP UP | WORLD CUP 2026

Group K is officially complete.

It delivered drama until the very end, with every qualification spot still on the line before the final whistle.

Final matchday results:

🇨🇴 Colombia 0:0 Portugal 🇵🇹
🇨🇩 DR Congo 3:1 Uzbekistan 🇺🇿

The teams advancing to the knockout stage are:

🇨🇴 Colombia, who will face Ghana in the Round of 32.

🇵🇹 Portugal, who finished second and will take on Croatia.

🇨🇩 DR Congo also advances as one of the best third placed teams.

🇺🇿 Uzbekistan ends its World Cup campaign without a single point.

DR Congo has been one of the biggest surprises of the group stage, while Colombia fully deserved to finish on top.

I'm also happy that my Polymarket prediction that Portugal would not win Group K cashed.
https://t.co/kOJOJV85vv

GROUP L WRAP UP | WORLD CUP 2026

Group L is officially complete.

The final matchday settled everything, with England and Croatia booking their places in the knockout stage.

The final standings:

🏴󠁧󠁢󠁥󠁮󠁧󠁿England finished top of the group with 7 points, living up to their status as pre tournament favorites.

🇭🇷 Croatia claimed second place with 6 points and also advanced to the Round of 32.

🇬🇭 Ghana finished third with 4 points and also qualified for the knockout stage as one of the best third placed teams.

🇵🇦 Panama ends its World Cup campaign without a single point.

England looked like the strongest team in the group from start to finish and deserved to finish in first place.

Unfortunately, my Polymarket prediction that England would not win Group L didn't cash.

Yesterday’s predictions: 2+ and 4-.

Overall Polymarket World Cup record: 33+ and 39-.

Another difficult day.

It felt like things could have gone much better, but again the small details went against me.

Colombia had a late goal disallowed, and Austria equalized in the very last seconds.

After that, there’s not much to say.

That’s football.

Quick recap:

> Messi and Ronaldo will not meet in the quarterfinals.

> The Uruguay Football Federation refused to fly the national team players out of the USA.

> Granit Xhaka has agreed terms with Chelsea.

> Messi scored his 19th goal at the World Cup.

WORLD CUP 2026: TODAY'S MATCHES COULD BE DECIDED BY CALCULATIONS... OR BY PURE AMBITION.
---------------------------
Algeria vs Austria.

Another fascinating situation.

A draw would be enough for both teams to reach the knockout stage, so it's easy to understand why many people are looking at that market.

But I'm not convinced.

I don't really believe in those conspiracy theories or the idea that teams simply agree to settle for a draw.

One moment of quality, one mistake, or one counterattack can completely change everything.

So this isn't really based on statistics or deep analysis.

It's just a feeling that one of these teams will find a way to win.

My Polymarket prediction: No draw.
----------------------------
Jordan vs Argentina.

Argentina have already secured first place in the group and their spot in the knockout stage, while Jordan have already been eliminated.

This is essentially a dead rubber, which should give Argentina the freedom to rotate the squad.

Messi is expected to start on the bench, so I'll be watching for his impact in the second half.

Hopefully someone else finds the net before then, because so far every Argentine goal at this World Cup has come from Messi. That streak has to end at some point...

Although I definitely wouldn't complain about another Messi hat-trick either.

My Polymarket prediction: Argentina to score 3+ goals.

WORLD CUP 2026: A SOLD OUT STADIUM, RONALDO, AND FIRST PLACE IN THE GROUP ON THE LINE.
-----------------------------
Colombia vs Portugal.

According to several reports, this is the most anticipated group stage match of the entire tournament, and it's easy to see why.

A sold out stadium.

Cristiano Ronaldo.

And first place in the group at stake.

Portugal has only one objective today.

Win.

Colombia, meanwhile, would be happy with a draw, which means they can stay patient and wait for opportunities on the counter.

Personally, I think Colombia looks like the more complete team right now, and their style is perfectly suited to punish Portugal when they push forward.

My Polymarket prediction: Colombia to win.
-------------------------------
DR Congo vs Uzbekistan.

Uzbekistan has had a disappointing tournament.

DR Congo, on the other hand, is just one win away from booking a historic place in the knockout stage.

They've impressed me throughout the group stage with their energy, discipline, and willingness to attack.

If they play at the same level today, I believe they can win comfortably and secure their spot in the Round of 32.

My Polymarket prediction: DR Congo to win by 2 or more goals.

GROUP G WRAP UP | WORLD CUP 2026

Group G is officially complete.

It came down to the very last matchday, but in the end Belgium held on to first place by goal difference.

The final standings:

🇧🇪 Belgium finished top of the group with 5 points.

🇪🇬 Egypt also collected 5 points and made history by reaching the World Cup knockout stage for the first time ever.

🇮🇷 Iran finished third with 3 points and still has a chance to qualify as one of the best third placed teams.

🇳🇿 New Zealand's World Cup journey comes to an end with just 1 point.

Egypt's qualification is one of the best stories from this group, while Belgium somehow managed to finish first despite making life much harder than it needed to be.

And yes... my Polymarket prediction on Belgium to win Group G somehow cashed in the end.

WORLD CUP 2026: THE FINAL GROUP STAGE MATCHES COULD BE DECIDED BY A SINGLE GOAL.
-------------------------------
Croatia vs Ghana.

This group is in a fascinating position because a draw would likely suit both teams.

That's why I'm expecting a very cautious match with few chances and even fewer goals.

Croatia has scored 2 and conceded 4 so far.

Ghana, on the other hand, has scored just once but hasn't conceded a single goal yet. One of the most unusual defensive records at this World Cup.

I don't think they'll abandon that approach today.

My Polymarket prediction: 2 goals or fewer.
--------------------------------
Panama vs England.

England have already secured their place in the knockout stage.

Panama, meanwhile, probably needs a huge win to have any realistic chance of advancing.

An interesting fact is that Panama is the only team at this World Cup that hasn't scored a goal yet.

Despite that, I actually think they deserved at least one in their previous matches.

Against a likely rotated and more relaxed England side, I believe they'll finally find the net.

My Polymarket prediction: Panama to score.

GROUP H WRAP UP | WORLD CUP 2026

Group H is officially complete.

Without a doubt, this was one of the biggest surprises of the entire group stage.

The final standings:

🇪🇸 Spain finish top of the group with 7 points.

🇨🇻 Cape Verde make history. In their first ever World Cup, they drew all three matches, finished with 3 points, and somehow that was enough to reach the Round of 32.

🇺🇾 Uruguay became the biggest disappointment of the group. With just 2 points, they finished third and became the first team at this World Cup to be officially eliminated despite finishing third.

🇸🇦 Saudi Arabia also ended the tournament with 2 points, finishing bottom of the group.

Cape Verde's story is exactly why the World Cup is so special.

Before this tournament, many fans had barely heard of them. Now they're heading to the knockout stage.

I'm also happy that my Polymarket prediction on Spain to win Group H cashed.

One thing prediction markets have been missing for a long time is a good mobile experience.

Most of us spend more time on our phones than on our laptops anyway.

Finally gave @PolycoolApp a try and honestly the app feels really clean.

The fact that you can deposit with Apple Pay makes getting started ridiculously easy.

Small details like this make a much bigger difference than people realize.

GROUP I WRAP UP | WORLD CUP 2026

Group I is officially complete.

France went through the group stage in dominant fashion and finished with a perfect record.

Final matchday results:

🇳🇴 Norway 1:4 France 🇫🇷
🇸🇳 Senegal 5:0 Iraq 🇮🇶

The teams advancing to the knockout stage are:
🇫🇷 France
🇳🇴 Norway

🇸🇳 Senegal still has a chance to qualify for the Round of 32 as one of the best third placed teams. After a 5:0 win over Iraq, they did everything they possibly could.

🇮🇶 Iraq's World Cup journey comes to an end.

France won all three group stage matches, took 9 points, and fully deserved to finish first.

Unfortunately, my Polymarket prediction that France would not win Group I didn't cash.

2/2

In my last post, I said I’d show you the cuisine that keeps making me come back for more.

It’s Georgian cuisine. 🇬🇪

Until about a year ago, I had barely tried it. Since then, I’ve visited four Georgian restaurants in four different cities.

The interesting part is that every place feels completely different.

Different interiors, different atmosphere, different presentation.

But somehow, they all make you feel a little bit closer to Georgia.

My favorites so far are khinkali, khachapuri, the soups and, of course, chacha.

The best part is that I still haven’t tried everything. Every visit feels like there’s something new waiting for me.

One thing that still surprises me is that my hometown doesn’t have a single Georgian restaurant. I honestly think it would be a success.

And somewhere along the way, this cuisine gave me a new goal.

One day I want to visit Georgia itself.

I want to taste these dishes where they were born and see how different they are from what I’ve experienced here.

People often say that the best way to discover a country is through its food.

Hopefully, one day I’ll find out for myself. 🇬🇪❤️

Yesterday’s predictions: 2+ and 4-.

Overall Polymarket World Cup record: 31+ and 35-.

Another disappointing day.

My reads just aren’t going the right way at the moment. I’m doing my best, but the results simply aren’t there yet.

Quick recap:

> Federico Valverde was in tears after being substituted.

> Lionel Messi will start the match against Jordan on the bench.

> Reece James has suffered another injury.

> Nico Paz has officially returned to Como.

> Ecuador’s president, Daniel Noboa, declared Friday a national holiday after Ecuador secured qualification for the World Cup knockout stage.

WORLD CUP 2026: TODAY SOME TEAMS FIGHT FOR FIRST PLACE, OTHERS ARE PLAYING FOR THEIR SURVIVAL.
---------------------------------
Belgium vs New Zealand.

If Belgium can't win this one, I honestly don't know what they're doing at this World Cup.

On paper, they landed in one of the easiest groups, yet somehow managed to make life incredibly difficult for themselves.

But the job is far from done.

New Zealand has a real chance to pull off another surprise, and they'll fight for every ball knowing what's at stake.

I don't expect an easy afternoon for Belgium.

My Polymarket prediction: New Zealand not to lose by 2 or more goals.
-----------------------------------
Egypt vs Iran.

Egypt have essentially secured their place in the knockout stage, so they'll play without much pressure.

Iran, on the other hand, has only one option.

Win.

That means they'll have to push numbers forward and take risks from the very beginning.

If that happens, Egypt will get exactly the kind of game they like, with space to attack on the counter.

And it could also be another opportunity for Mohamed Salah to improve his World Cup numbers.

My Polymarket prediction: 2 or more goals.

WORLD CUP 2026: THE MOST DRAMATIC GROUP OF THE TOURNAMENT WILL BE DECIDED TODAY. EVERY GOAL COULD CHANGE THE STANDINGS.
--------------------------------
Cape Verde vs Saudi Arabia.

A direct battle that could decide not only third place, but potentially even first depending on the other result.

At this point, it's impossible not to root for Cape Verde.

They've become one of the best stories of this World Cup, and I think almost the entire football world will be cheering for them today.

If they win, there's a real chance they could even finish in the top two.

Hopefully Vozinha keeps another clean sheet, and the attack finds the goal they've been fighting for.

My Polymarket prediction: Cape Verde to win.
--------------------------------
Uruguay vs Spain.

Uruguay have only one option today.

They must win.

Honestly, that's a huge challenge against this Spanish side.

There have been plenty of rumors about problems inside the Uruguayan camp. Whether they're true or not, we'll find out soon enough.

One thing I'm sure about is that this should be a fantastic match. Uruguay will have to attack from the first whistle, while Yamal and the rest of Spain's attack will definitely create their chances.

It has all the ingredients for an entertaining game.

My Polymarket prediction: Both teams to score.

Been supporting Cape Verde since childhood.

Spain are the favorites to win the group.

But this is the World Cup.

> There have been plenty of rumors about tension between Uruguay's coach and the squad. Whether it's true or not, we'll find out soon enough.

> Cape Verde already is one of the biggest surprises of the tournament. Before this World Cup, many people didn't even know this national team existed.

So, just for fun, I placed a Polymarket bet on Cape Verde to win the group.

Is it realistic?

Probably not.

But why not?

WORLD CUP 2026: TODAY WE FIND OUT WHO FINISHES FIRST AND WHO SAYS GOODBYE TO THE TOURNAMENT.
-----------------------------
Norway vs France.

Both teams have already secured their place in the knockout stage, and today's match will decide who wins the group.

I expect both managers to rotate their squads and give some key players a chance to rest before the Round of 32.

France will also be without Didier Deschamps on the touchline, and even if the quality is still there, his absence could have an impact.

As for Norway, I expect Erling Haaland to be fully motivated. The Golden Boot race is heating up, and every goal matters at this stage of the tournament.

My Polymarket prediction: France not to win.
------------------------------
Iraq vs Senegal.

Both teams are sitting on zero points, but mathematically the door is still slightly open.

The problem is that one of them likely needs to win by at least three goals to have any realistic chance of reaching the knockout stage as one of the best third placed teams.

I believe Senegal has the quality to do it.

Their squad is simply stronger on paper, and with the right mentality from the first minute, they have everything needed to produce a convincing win.

My Polymarket prediction: Senegal to score 3 or more goals.

GROUP F WRAP UP | WORLD CUP 2026

Group F is officially complete.

One of the most entertaining groups of the tournament finished without any major surprises. In the end, the teams finished almost exactly where most people expected them to before the World Cup began.

The teams advancing to the knockout stage are:

🇳🇱 Netherlands
🇯🇵 Japan

🇸🇪 Sweden also advances to the Round of 32 as one of the best third placed teams.

🇹🇳 Tunisia's World Cup journey comes to an end without a single point.

Now the real tournament begins.

The knockout stage, where there is no room for mistakes.

Personally, I believed Japan would win this group. They played some of the best football of the entire group stage, but in the end they had to settle for second place.

That also means my Polymarket prediction on Japan to win Group F didn't cash.

GROUP E WRAP UP | WORLD CUP 2026

Group E is officially complete.

This was one of the most dramatic groups of the tournament, with the race for qualification going right down to the final seconds.

Final matchday results:

🇪🇨 Ecuador 2:1 Germany 🇩🇪
🇨🇼 Curacao 0:2 Ivory Coast 🇨🇮

The teams advancing to the knockout stage are:
🇩🇪 Germany
🇨🇮 Ivory Coast

🇪🇨 Ecuador also advances to the Round of 32 as one of the best third placed teams.

🇨🇼 Curacao's World Cup journey comes to an end.

Until the final whistle, nobody knew who would qualify from this group. That's exactly why I love the World Cup. 🌍❤️

Despite losing their final match, Germany held on to first place thanks to a superior goal difference.

I'm also happy that my Polymarket prediction on Germany to win Group E cashed.

GROUP D WRAP UP | WORLD CUP 2026

Group D is officially complete.

Without a doubt, this was one of the biggest surprises of the entire group stage.

Final matchday results:

🇵🇾 Paraguay 0:0 Australia 🇦🇺
🇹🇷 Turkey 3:2 United States 🇺🇸

The teams advancing to the knockout stage are:
🇺🇸 United States
🇦🇺 Australia

🇵🇾 Paraguay also advances to the Round of 32 as one of the best third placed teams.

🇹🇷 Turkey's World Cup journey comes to an end.

The biggest disappointment of this group for me was Turkey.

Before the tournament started, I really believed in this generation of Turkish players, but they never lived up to the expectations and were eliminated in the group stage.

Unfortunately, that also means my Polymarket prediction on Turkey to win Group D didn't cash.
